home.title: Mamoru Yamada
home.subtitle: 山田 守
home.description: There are buildings we use casually and pass by without a second thought — school classrooms, buildings, campuses. These familiar environments may be extraordinary spaces woven together by the ingenuity, effort, and encounters of those who came before us. We decided to focus on an architect we live close to but don't know well.
home.projectDescription: This project involves the production of a documentary film about architect Mamoru Yamada, who designed the Tokai University Shonan Campus. The film will document his architectural works and include interviews with related individuals, aiming to preserve and convey the appeal of Yamada's architecture to future generations.
home.studentRole: Student Involvement: As part of a course in the Department of Arts, School of Humanities and Culture, Tokai University, students will take the lead in all aspects of the project—from planning and research to video shooting, editing, title design, sound, and music production.
